Title: A conserved mechanism affecting hydride shifting and deprotonation in the synthesis of hopane triterpenes as compositions of wax in oat
Journal: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression in transient N. benthamiana expression assays
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. tHMGR from oat was coinfiltrated to boost triterpene production in the heterologous system. 2. It served as pathway support rather than the main experimental subject affecting product specificity. 3. It is not demonstrated as the decisive causal gene for wax hopane composition in oat in this paper.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as metabolic support in heterologous expression; not a primary core gene in Results.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana leaves

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triterpenoid biosynthesis; hopane-type triterpene branch of oxidosqualene cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene through dammarenyl and hopyl C22 cation intermediates, followed by direct deprotonation at C29 to form hopenol B
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining control step for hopane scaffold formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in young and mature panicles, highest in mature panicles
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In oat, panicle-enriched, highest in mature panicles; in transient N. benthamiana leaves, ectopically expressed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Surface wax component likely contributing to protection against drought or cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of hopenol B in oat panicle wax
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR structural identification; qRT-PCR tissue expression; wax extraction and GC-MS; site-directed mutagenesis; QM/MM modeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsHS1 (formerly AsOSC7) was identified as a hopenol B synthase. 2. It is highly expressed in mature panicles, matching detection of hopenol B in panicle surface wax. 3. Substitution of key active-site residues, especially Y410A with additional V121I/C722A, converted product specificity toward hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol. 4. C366 functions as a general base for deprotonation in hopenol B formation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome sequencing/RNA-seq from A. strigosa tissues (accession not given here; raw data referenced to previously published paper); genome sequence A. strigosa S75 v8.0
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Surface wax analyzed from 2-mo-old oat tissues; heterologous transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate enzyme to engineer hopane-type wax triterpenes in cereals for altered cuticular chemistry; product specificity can be rationally tuned by residue editing at positions 121/410/722
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential wax-composition shifts may affect cuticle properties; no direct growth penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AsHS1 corresponds to AsOSC7. Residue Y410 is pivotal in suppressing rearrangement by steric effects limiting cation–π interaction with F257; F257 and F729 are mechanistically important residues; C366D retains activity whereas C366G is inactive.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triterpenoid biosynthesis; hopane-type triterpene branch of oxidosqualene cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene through hopyl C22 cation followed by hydride shift/rearrangement and deprotonation at C17/C21-positioning to form hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key branch-determining control step for hopane scaffold rearrangement outcome
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very strongly expressed in sheathes and also in shoots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In oat, sheath/shoot-enriched; in transient N. benthamiana leaves, ectopically expressed
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Surface wax component likely contributing to protection against drought or cold stress
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes biosynthesis of hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol in oat sheath wax
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS; NMR structural identification; qRT-PCR tissue expression; wax extraction and GC-MS; site-directed mutagenesis; QM/MM modeling
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AsHS2 (formerly AsOSC8) was identified as a hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol synthase. 2. It is highly expressed in sheathes, matching detection of hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol in sheath surface wax. 3. Residue A410 is critical for enabling rearrangement via cation–π interaction with F257. 4. Reverse mutagenesis did not fully convert AsHS2 into a pure hopenol B synthase but generated isomotiol and/or mixed products depending on residue combinations.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome sequencing/RNA-seq from A. strigosa tissues (accession not given here; raw data referenced to previously published paper); genome sequence A. strigosa S75 v8.0
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Surface wax analyzed from 2-mo-old oat tissues; heterologous transient Agrobacterium expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering specific hopane-type wax triterpenes; residue-level editing can tune product profile but reverse conversion may also generate isomotiol side products
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential off-target alteration of wax chemistry and side-product isomotiol formation; no direct agronomic penalty reported
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AsHS2 corresponds to AsOSC8. A410Y single mutation enables isomotiol formation, while combined I121V/A410Y/A722C changes activity toward hopenol B plus isomotiol rather than full clean conversion.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Triterpenoid biosynthesis; hopane-type triterpene branch of oxidosqualene cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes 2,3-oxidosqualene to predominantly form hopenol B; corresponding residue L413 constrains active-site geometry analogous to AsHS1 Y410
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently expressed in N. benthamiana leaves
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Hopane-type triterpenes; specific native biological role not experimentally tested here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces hopenol B; L413A-containing mutants gain hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol production, supporting a conserved mechanism across monocots and dicots
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene synthesis; transient expression in N. benthamiana; GC-MS; site-directed mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AcHS1 is a hopenol B synthase from a basal dicot. 2. Mutagenesis at residues corresponding to AsHS1/AsHS2 positions showed that L413 is crucial for constraining product specificity. 3. L413A-containing double and triple mutants produced hop-17(21)-en-3β-ol, supporting a conserved catalytic mechanism.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Predicted from Phytozome A. coerulea genome v3.1
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Transient Agrobacterium-mediated expression in N. benthamiana
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides a dicot ortholog for comparative engineering of hopane-type triterpene cyclases and mechanistic residue design
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used mainly to validate conservation of catalytic residue logic rather than native wax function in Aquilegia.
